name: Apify Platform API
what_it_does: Cloud platform for running web scraping Actors — containerized, scalable scraping functions — with 2,000+ ready-made Actors for popular sites and a REST API for orchestration.
use_cases: Scraping LinkedIn profiles, job listings, or company data for agent research tasks
Extracting product data, reviews, and pricing from Amazon and e-commerce sites
Running Google Maps, SERP, or social media scrapers without building custom infrastructure
Orchestrating multi-step data collection pipelines via the Apify API
Using community Actors to quickly add data sources to an agent without writing scrapers
not_for: Real-time low-latency data needs (Actor startup adds seconds of overhead)
Sites explicitly prohibiting scraping where legal risk is a concern
Simple single-page data extraction where a direct HTTP fetch would suffice
best_when: An agent needs structured data from a complex website (LinkedIn, Amazon, Google Maps) and a ready-made Actor already exists for that target.
avoid_when: You need sub-second data retrieval, real-time streaming, or the target site has an official API that should be used instead.

auth.notes: API token passed as a Bearer token or via ?token= query parameter. Tokens are scoped to a single user account with full access; no fine-grained permission scopes available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $5 platform credit per month (resets monthly); enough for ~100 Actor runs depending on compute usage
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter at $49/month (includes more credits); scale tiers for higher usage; compute billed per Actor CU (Compute Unit)
pricing.notes: Credit consumption depends heavily on Actor complexity; lightweight scrapers cost fractions of a cent per run while browser-based Actors cost significantly more.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits: 200 req/min for most endpoints; Actor run concurrency depends on plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors are well-structured JSON with codes and messages. Actor-level errors vary by Actor author quality. Webhook failure notifications are available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Actor runs are triggered as async jobs; agents must poll for completion or use webhooks. Re-running an Actor creates a new run, not idempotent by default. Dataset reads are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Actor runs are asynchronous; agents must implement polling or webhook handling to get results
Ready-made Actor quality varies widely — always evaluate the Actor before deploying to production
Actor startup time (cold start) adds 5-30 seconds of latency before scraping begins
Browser-based Actors consume significantly more compute credits than lightweight HTTP scrapers
Some popular Actors (LinkedIn, etc.) may be blocked or rate-limited by target sites and require proxy rotation
MCP server is community/Apify-maintained; verify current stability before relying on it in production

security_score_components.security_notes: Actors run in isolated Docker containers. No fine-grained API key scopes. GDPR-compliant data storage available in EU region. Actor marketplace code is third-party and should be reviewed before use.
